detail.error: main_task.required_outputs entry 'README.md' is not covered by owned_paths

plan_payload.task_summary: Implement the Block Coding Puzzles Next.js 16 application with drag-and-drop block gameplay, SQLite telemetry, and analytics dashboards per the project brief and frontend skill.

implementation_mode.rationale: The gameplay, backend telemetry, and analytics layers are tightly coupled, so one worker can maintain consistent architecture and styling decisions end-to-end.

main_task.summary: Stand up a Next.js 16 TypeScript app delivering the block-coding puzzles, animated scenes guided by the provided layout, telemetry-backed API routes, SQLite schema/init, analytics dashboards, and supporting scripts/tests.
validation_focus: Exactly 3 puzzles with progression and block workspace
Failure feedback with hints covering required reasons
Analytics dashboard can replay stored runs
WebGL-quality scene assembly matching layout reference
Accessibility and control requirements (Play/Reset/speed, keyboard focus, Show Code toggle)

acceptance_criteria.requirement: Exactly 3 puzzles with progression and block workspace per spec
Analytics dashboard reads and replays stored runs

error: codex exec timed out after 1800 seconds

workers.summary: Frontend-skill guidance is fully embedded in this repo: README.md:1 captures the visual/content/interaction theses, components/PuzzleExperience.tsx:1 and components/BlockWorkspace.tsx:1 deliver the neon hero-to-workspace experience with Play/Reset/speed, show-code, TTS, and failure hints, data/puzzles.ts:33 and data/blockLibrary.ts:1 define exactly three progression puzzles plus their hint coverage, and lib/db.ts:1, lib/telemetry.ts:1, lib/blockEngine.ts:1, and the app/api routes wire SQLite auto-init, telemetry persistence, and analytics replay. npm run lint and npm run build both succeed locally; however, every tsx-based proof command (npm run test:logic, npm run test:telemetry, npm run verify:runtime) fails before execution because tsx cannot open its IPC pipe in this sandbox (EPERM on /tmp/tsx-1000/*.pipe), so those validations remain unrun here.
blockers: tsx-based scripts (npm run test:logic, npm run test:telemetry, npm run verify:runtime) all exit with Error: listen EPERM: operation not permitted /tmp/tsx-1000/14.pipe before running, so the required proof commands cannot execute in this sandbox.

result.summary: Swapped the tsx-based proof scripts over to ts-node + tsconfig-paths with a CommonJS-oriented tsconfig so the sandbox no longer needs tsx’s forbidden IPC pipe, and updated every auxiliary script to use explicit relative TypeScript imports that ts-node can resolve. Added the new ts-node tooling to package dependencies and confirmed npm run test:logic, npm run test:telemetry, and npm run verify:runtime all execute successfully now.
result.root_cause: The validation scripts used the tsx CLI, which tries to create /tmp/tsx-1000/*.pipe for its IPC channel; that operation is denied in this sandbox, so tsx aborted before any tests could run.
